page: Microsoft Azure M64-16ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The server is equipped with a massive 1792.0 GB of RAM, translating to 112.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 2097 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff between compute power and memory capacity. While the server delivers average multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 10937.09 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5481.0, its single-core performance is weak, scoring 1038.28 in stress-ng and 887.0 in Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 609.78 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 75.32 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though the Passmark memory score is weak at 1779.75.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads that require extreme memory density rather than raw single-threaded processing power. With 10.8 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and average database operation performance, the server is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. However, it is poorly suited for single-threaded compression tasks or LLM text generation, where it achieves only 6.05 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.
description: Microsoft Azure M64-16ms is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 8 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and a 2.7 GHz clock speed. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it features an exceptional 1792.0 GB of RAM alongside 2097 GB of local storage. Benchmarks reveal a distinct performance tradeoff: while multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are average, single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 887.0 and poor single-threaded compression results. This configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications rather than compute-heavy tasks. It is highly suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads, but is less effective for single-threaded processing or LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64-16ms is a memory-optimized VM featuring 16 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8280M processor, 1792 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64-16ms is a memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 1792 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage, built for large in-memory databases.
tagline: An ultra-high-memory virtual machine featuring exceptional RAM density and local storage for memory-bound enterprise applications and large-scale database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Massive 1792.0 GB of RAM providing 112.0 GB per core
Includes 2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance but weak single-core execution speeds
Optimized for large in-memory databases and Redis caching layers
